REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. THE COURT REQUIRED PLAINTIFF TO PERSONALLY SERVE THE COMPLAINT, SUMMONS, AND MOVING PAPERS. PLAINTIFF'S SUBSTITUTE SERVICE WITHOUT A DECLARATION OF DILIGENCE IS THUS IMPROPER. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE ARE SEVERE ISSUES WITH THE PURPORTED LEASE'S CREDIBILITY BASED ON THE LEASE'S BREVITY AND CONTENT, THE FACT THAT THE PROPERTY OWNER DID NOT SIGN IT, PLAINTIFF'S TEXT MESSAGES THAT A JUDGE "LOOKED OVER IT," AND THE MISMATCH IN DEFENDANT'S SIGNATURES. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ELISE YAMAGUCHI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS QUASHED. AS THIS COURT ALREADY RULED ON JUNE 10, 2025, WITH REGARD TO THE OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, "THE COURT REQUIRED PLAINTIFF TO PERSONALLY SERVE THE COMPLAINT, SUMMONS, AND MOVING PAPERS. PLAINTIFF'S SUBSTITUTE SERVICE WITHOUT A DECLARATION OF DILIGENCE IS THUS IMPROPER." THE COURT ALSO FINDS THAT THE PURPORTED SERVICE TO A POST BOX INC. ADDRESS IN PACIFICA WAS IMPROPER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: VICTOR M. HWANG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2025 Continued To Dec-10-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ELISE YAMAGUCHI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Service of summons is quashed. As this Court already ruled on June 10, 2025, with regard to the OSC Re: Preliminary Injunction, "the Court required Plaintiff to personally serve the Complaint, Summons, and moving papers. Plaintiff's substitute service without a declaration of diligence is thus improper." The Court also finds that the purported service to a Post Box Inc. address in Pacifica was improper. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Victor M. Hwang, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/VMH)

OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is DENIED. The Court required Plaintiff to personally serve the Complaint, Summons, and moving papers. Plaintiff's substitute service without a declaration of diligence is thus improper. Moreover, Plaintiff has not shown a likelihood of success on the merits. The Court notes that there are severe issues with the purported lease's credibility based on the lease's brevity and content, the fact that the property owner did not sign it, Plaintiff's text messages that a judge "looked over it," and the mismatch in Defendant's signatures.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
